description: Post-pandemic, San Francisco is facing a severe housing shortage and high commercial office vacancy. Identify prime candidates for 'adaptive reuse'—converting underutilized, older non-residential buildings into residential apartments. Using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/adaptive_reuse.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, identify the top 100 largest buildings that are strictly non-residential (0 units), have at least 20,000 non-residential square feet, were built before 1990, and are underutilized (defined as having 0 total jobs or a square-footage-per-job ratio greater than 400). Export the list, sorted by non-residential square footage in descending order, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/adaptive_reuse_candidates.csv including the columns building_id, year_built, non_residential_sqft, total_jobs, and sqft_per_job. Additionally, save a scatter plot of these candidates showing year_built versus non_residential_sqft to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/reuse_scatter.png and ensure all notebook cells are executed.
